scenarios.task: Facility A self-reports several exceedances this year through its rigorous monitoring program. Facility B reports none. I'm treating Facility A as the worse compliance risk since it has more documented exceedances on record.
criteria: Flags treating the self-reporting facility as worse based on exceedance count alone as the failure mode
States that a facility with rigorous monitoring will surface more excursions on paper than one with minimal monitoring hiding the same or worse performance
Recommends verifying each specific finding rather than treating self-reported exceedance counts as a proxy for worse compliance culture
Notes that treating high self-reported counts as automatically worse discourages monitoring, getting the incentive backwards

scenarios.task: This facility was classified as a small quantity generator last year. I'm applying that same classification to this month's inspection without pulling this month's actual hazardous waste generation records.
criteria: Flags applying last year's classification without checking current records as the failure mode
States that generator status is a monthly determination based on actual generation records, not a classification that sticks
Recommends pulling the actual monthly generation records for the current inspection period before determining status
Notes that a facility can shift generator categories month to month with production volume changes

scenarios.task: I'm calculating a penalty for a confirmed permit violation based solely on the gravity/severity of the violation, without including an economic-benefit component for the cost the facility avoided by delaying compliance.
criteria: Flags calculating penalty on gravity alone as the failure mode
States that a penalty has two separable components (economic benefit and gravity), and omitting economic benefit lets the facility keep the financial benefit of having violated
Recommends calculating and adding an economic-benefit component (time-value-of-money adjusted avoided/delayed cost) to the gravity component
States that a gravity-only penalty has no mechanism to remove the financial incentive that led to the delay

scenarios.task: I issued a Notice of Violation to a facility, but I'm treating it informally as a heads-up letter rather than tracking whether the facility actually meets the specific compliance schedule deadline stated in it.
criteria: Flags treating the NOV as informal/low-stakes rather than a formal legal step as the failure mode
States that an NOV is a formal legal step with a response deadline, and how the facility responds within that schedule genuinely affects whether the matter proceeds to formal enforcement
Recommends actively tracking the facility's response against the stated compliance schedule deadline
Notes that misreading the NOV as low-stakes paperwork happens from either the inspector's or facility's side, and both are wrong
